issues.body: The `republish-trust` step runs after the model and local gate, but passes `$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/target/scripts/trust-gate.py`. The model runs with `target` as its working tree and may legitimately or maliciously modify that path, especially for a trust-surface issue. Thus the candidate change controls the trust-gate program used to authorize its own publish. Gate success does not restore the file or bind the verifier to the pre-model/base revision, so a weakened or replaced script can return `trusted`/`promoted` and make this last-step check succeed. This defeats the stated independent live trust revalidation.
issues.fix_hint: Run the recheck with an immutable verifier obtained from a checkout/ref that the model cannot modify (for example, a separate read-only checkout pinned to the resolved base SHA), while still querying the live issue. Add a self-test/assertion that the republish step's `--trust-gate` path is rooted in that immutable checkout and cannot resolve into the model's `target` tree.
issues.title: Pre-publish reverification executes a model-mutable trust gate

issues.body: The added checks establish only that the publish step references `republish-trust` and that `republish-trust` references gate success. They pass even when `--trust-gate` points at the post-model mutable target checkout, so they do not turn red for the security failure introduced by this diff.
issues.fix_hint: Extend the self-test to assert that the reverify command uses the designated immutable, SHA-bound checkout's trust-gate and explicitly rejects `$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/target/scripts/trust-gate.py` (or any equivalent path in the model-mutable tree).
issues.title: Workflow self-test misses the trust-root property

verdict.summary: The new publish-time check is fail-open with respect to the code implementing the check: it executes trust-gate.py from the target working tree after the model has modified that tree. A worker change can therefore alter the verifier that is supposed to authorize its own publication. The workflow assertions only check step conditions and do not detect this trust-root substitution.
